briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than actual artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting the risks of uninsured fintech banking products.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products into increasingly niche and unconventional territory, reshaping how consumers think about credit and rewards.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and consumer lending.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape and what it means for incumbent financial institutions.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a milestone still shaping Canadian fintech regulation today.

- **[CVCA and NACO offer competing visions for feds' $750-million venture envelope](https://betakit.com/cvca-and-naco-offer-competing-visions-for-feds-750-million-venture-envelope/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's two leading venture capital associations are presenting divergent strategies to government on how best to deploy a $750-million federal venture funding envelope, with major implications for fintech and tech startups nationwide.

- **[UniUni raises $85 million USD as it scales gig-powered delivery across North America](https://betakit.com/uniuni-raises-85-million-usd-as-it-scales-gig-powered-delivery-across-north-america/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian last-mile delivery startup UniUni has secured $85 million USD to expand its gig-economy-powered delivery network, reflecting continued investor appetite for Canadian tech infrastructure plays.

- **[How Gaiia used its R&D to help finance a pivot from services to software](https://betakit.com/how-gaiia-used-its-rd-to-help-finance-a-pivot-from-services-to-software/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian startup Gaiia leveraged R&D financing mechanisms to fund a strategic pivot from services to a scalable software model, offering a useful case study for Atlantic Canadian founders exploring non-dilutive funding.

- **[Canadians feel the pinch at the pump as conflict in the Middle East drives up gas prices](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/gas-prices-soar-canada-middle-east-conflict-9.7118590?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Escalating conflict in the Middle East is pushing fuel costs higher for Canadians, adding inflationary pressure that could influence Bank of Canada rate decisions and consumer financial behaviour.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[CVCA and NACO offer competing visions for feds' $750-million venture envelope](https://betakit.com/cvca-and-naco-offer-competing-visions-for-feds-750-million-venture-envelope/)** — *BetaKit* — The debate over how Canada deploys its $750-million venture envelope is directly relevant to Atlantic fintech founders and ecosystem builders seeking access to federal venture capital programs.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — As Canada's open banking framework continues to evolve, Atlantic credit unions and fintech startups should stay engaged with how regulatory timelines and API standards will affect data-sharing obligations.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — This analysis of how open banking drives payments competition is essential reading for Atlantic Canadian credit unions and embedded finance players preparing for a more open financial ecosystem.
